nodes.label: API Layer: Presigned URLs, Job Management, Rate Limiting
nodes.norm_label: api layer: presigned urls, job management, rate limiting

nodes.label: Cloud Run Conversion Backend
nodes.rationale: Planned or in-development backend service for HWP-to-PDF conversion. Referenced in root scripts as dev:api and build:api but may not be present in current workspace. Uses async job-based model processing.

nodes.label: Cloud Run Document Conversion Architecture
nodes.rationale: Three-tier serverless architecture with Next.js frontend on Vercel, Cloud Run API/Worker for conversion, and GCS temp bucket. Offloads heavy conversion workloads from Vercel function limits (4.5MB/6MB payload).

nodes.label: Documentation Gaps
nodes.rationale: Missing root README.md, default Create Next App README in apps/web, and unverified root scripts (api, typecheck, test). Backend conversion service (apps/api) not found during init pass.

nodes.label: GCS Lifecycle Management
nodes.rationale: Automatic cleanup of temporary files via GCS bucket lifecycle policies. Path-based rules target temp/original/ and temp/result/ prefixes for deletion after 1 day, plus AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload rule.

nodes.label: GCS Lifecycle Rules
nodes.rationale: Bucket-level policies in Google Cloud Storage for automatic object management via Delete, SetStorageClass, and AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload actions. Configuration in infrastructure/gcp/bucket-lifecycle.json.

nodes.label: H2Orestart Extension
nodes.rationale: Community LibreOffice extension (ebandal/H2Orestart, GPL-3.0, v0.7.12) that provides HWP v5.0 and HWPX support. Installed via unopkg add --shared, invoked with --infilter=Hwp2002_File. Required because built-in LibreOffice HWP filter has silent corruption bugs.

nodes.label: HWP File Format Conversion Challenges
nodes.rationale: HWP is more complex than DOCX/PDF. LibreOffice built-in filter only supports v3.0 with v5.0 silent corruption (tdf#70097). H2Orestart community extension is the operational solution. High-risk: 30-60s cold start delays, OOM on large HWP files, cost leakage from incomplete multipart uploads.

nodes.label: HWP File Upload Constraints
nodes.rationale: Centralized in packages/shared/src/index.ts. Only .hwp files accepted, max 20MB, async job/status response types, and polling constants. Single source of truth for upload validation.

nodes.label: HWP File Upload Validation
nodes.rationale: Validation logic for HWP uploads centralized in packages/shared/src/index.ts. Enforces .hwp extension and 20MB size limit, with async job/status response types and polling constants.

nodes.label: HWP2PDF Project
nodes.rationale: Serverless web service converting HWP to PDF. pnpm monorepo with Next.js frontend (apps/web) and Cloud Run backend. Async job-based workflow with LibreOffice headless conversion on Cloud Run.

nodes.label: HWP2PDF Service Architecture
nodes.rationale: Three-tier serverless cloud-native design: Next.js frontend on Vercel, Cloud Run API Gateway, GCS temp bucket, Cloud Run Converter Worker. Presigned URL direct uploads, UUID-based naming, Upstash Redis rate limiting, 2-second polling interval.

nodes.label: @hwp2pdf/shared Package
nodes.rationale: Shared library at packages/shared, package name @hwp2pdf/shared, built with tsc. Centralizes .hwp file validation (20MB max), job status types, API routes, and polling constants for consistent use across frontend and backend.

nodes.label: LibreOffice Headless Conversion
nodes.rationale: LibreOffice 7.6.x in headless mode transforms HWP to PDF on server. Uses H2Orestart filter (--infilter=Hwp2002_File), Korean locale (ko_KR.UTF-8), 2GiB memory min, 3-min timeout. Cold starts 60+s mitigated by listener mode (unoserver/Gotenberg).

nodes.label: llm-wiki Documentation System
nodes.rationale: Repository-local documentation system for OpenCode sessions. Entry point docs/INDEX.md, with AGENTS.md for compact agent guidance, superpowers/specs/ for plans, docs/sessions/ for dated notes. Verified facts only.

nodes.label: llm-wiki Repository Structure
nodes.rationale: pnpm monorepo with apps/* and packages/* globs. Root package.json specifies pnpm@8.15.0 and Node >=20. apps/web is Next.js 16.2.9 frontend, packages/shared is @hwp2pdf/shared tsc-built library. No root README.md.

nodes.label: Monorepo Workspace Scripts
nodes.rationale: Root package.json declares workspace-level scripts. pnpm -r build verified 2026-06-13 building packages/shared (tsc) then apps/web (next build). Topological order enforced by pnpm.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 Frontend Package
nodes.rationale: Frontend in apps/web, package name web, framework Next.js 16.2.9. Integrates @hwp2pdf/shared for validation/types. Has its own apps/web/AGENTS.md for framework-specific guidance. Built with next build.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 Frontend
nodes.rationale: Primary client app named web in apps/web/. Built on Next.js 16.2.9, TypeScript, pnpm@8.15.0, Node >=20. Consumes @hwp2pdf/shared for validation, types, API routes, and polling constants.

nodes.label: Next.js 16.2.9 Frontend
nodes.rationale: Web app at apps/web, package name web, framework Next.js 16.2.9. Source under apps/web/src/app and apps/web/src/components with @/* alias from apps/web/tsconfig.json. apps/web/AGENTS.md applies for changes.

nodes.label: Next.js Frontend Application
nodes.rationale: Primary user-facing component at apps/web. Next.js 16.2.9 with source in apps/web/src/app and apps/web/src/components. Depends on @hwp2pdf/shared for validation, types, and API routes.

nodes.label: pnpm Monorepo Structure
nodes.rationale: Workspace-based repo organization with apps/* and packages/* globs. Root package.json has workspaces, private:true, packageManager pnpm@8.15.0. apps/web (Next.js Vercel), apps/api (Cloud Run), packages/shared (types).

nodes.label: pnpm Monorepo Workspace Configuration
nodes.rationale: pnpm-workspace.yaml declares apps/* and packages/* globs. Root package.json has pnpm@8.15.0, Node >=20. apps/web (Next.js 16.2.9, web) and packages/shared (@hwp2pdf/shared, tsc) are the verified packages.

nodes.label: pnpm Recursive Build Verification
nodes.rationale: Recursive build verification confirms the workspace dependency graph resolves correctly and each package's build tooling executes properly. The -r flag runs pnpm across all workspace packages in dependency order, ensuring packages with dependencies are built after their dependencies.

nodes.label: pnpm Workspace Architecture
nodes.rationale: Monorepo structure with pnpm workspaces enables code sharing between frontend and backend while maintaining clear boundaries between application layers. The apps/* and packages/* pattern separates deployable applications from internal libraries.

nodes.label: pnpm Workspace Configuration
nodes.rationale: Standard pnpm workspace layout uses apps/* and packages/* globs in pnpm-workspace.yaml. Root package.json pins packageManager to pnpm@8.15.0 and requires Node >=20 for consistent tooling across the workspace.

nodes.label: pnpm Workspace Monorepo
nodes.rationale: pnpm workspace monorepo enables centralized dependency management, shared tooling, and atomic commits across related projects. The workspace: protocol ensures local package changes are immediately reflected without publishing to a registry.

nodes.label: Presigned URL Security Pattern
nodes.rationale: Defense-in-depth is achieved through time-bounded access (5-min upload TTL, 15-min download TTL), content-type restrictions, UUID-based object key isolation, and optional IP/User-Agent restrictions. The GCS bucket remains private; only signed URLs grant access.

nodes.label: Presigned URL Upload Pattern
nodes.rationale: The pattern bypasses Vercel's serverless function body size limits (4.5MB Hobby, 6MB Pro) by having browsers upload directly to GCS. HWP files with embedded images commonly exceed these limits, making presigned URLs the only viable upload mechanism.

nodes.label: Redis Job Queue System
nodes.rationale: Redis-based job tracking with 2-hour TTL is sufficient because the Cloud Run conversion worker runs with concurrency=1 (LibreOffice is single-process), so no additional queue synchronization is needed. TTL-based cleanup eliminates the need for a dedicated cleanup worker.

nodes.label: Serverless Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: In-memory rate limiting fails in serverless because each function instance has independent memory. A user with 60 req/hr limit can effectively use 180 req/hr across 3 instances. Upstash Redis HTTP API is the recommended solution because it avoids persistent connections.

nodes.label: Session-Based Documentation
nodes.rationale: Dated session notes provide a verifiable record of what was actually executed and succeeded, complementing the principle of preferring verified executable sources over potentially outdated documentation.

nodes.label: Session Documentation Rule
nodes.rationale: Mandatory session note creation after successful code/config changes maintains a searchable history for both human contributors and AI assistants. Conciseness is prioritized over verbosity.

nodes.label: Shared Package Architecture
nodes.rationale: Centralizing utilities in a single shared package reduces duplication, ensures consistency across consumers, and propagates changes (e.g., file size limits) automatically to all dependents.

nodes.label: Shared TypeScript Contracts Package
nodes.rationale: TypeScript Compiler is sufficient as the build tool because the package primarily exports types and utility functions, not complex bundled code. The dist/ output provides full type safety to consumers.

nodes.label: Shared TypeScript Contracts
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for TypeScript contracts prevents type drift between frontend and backend. The 20MB size limit and .hwp-only restriction are policy decisions enforced through shared code.

nodes.label: Upstash Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: Upstash Redis HTTP API is serverless-friendly (no persistent connections needed). The sliding window algorithm provides balanced accuracy and the 60 req/hr limit is per IP for abuse prevention. X-Forwarded-For handling is critical behind proxies.

nodes.label: Vercel OIDC Federation
nodes.rationale: OIDC Federation eliminates the need to store, rotate, and secure static Service Account key files. Short-lived, automatically-rotated tokens reduce attack surface. Audience claim must exactly match Cloud Run URL for security.

nodes.label: Verified Build Commands
nodes.rationale: Commands must be verified as working before being documented. Unverified scripts (dev:api, build:api, typecheck, test) should not be relied upon without first confirming the target package and script exist.
